title: Validate Renode nRF52840 with LICHEN test suite
description: Run existing LICHEN Zephyr tests on Renode nRF52840 target. Verify: boot, LoRa TX/RX via lichen-sim, L2 ping, basic mesh. Add to CI matrix if stable.
notes:
VALIDATION RESULTS (Renode 1.16.1 linux-arm64-portable-dotnet; T-Echo puck firmware)

Installed Renode 1.16.1 arm64 (needs DOTNET_SYSTEM_GLOBALIZATION_INVARIANT=1 when
libicu absent). Built lichen/apps/puck for t_echo/nrf52840 with the new
renode_console.overlay/conf (-DBOARD_ROOT, -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=lichen,
SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH). Ran single-node and 2-node harnesses against lichen-sim.

Four defects found and fixed (commit c3dd4d5 + df3cf92):
1. SX1262 was on spi2; firmware drives spi1 (0x40004000). Moved to spi1 and
   replaced Renode's stock twi1 (I2C) there with an SPI controller.
2. SPI controller needed easyDMA:true so the SPIM EasyDMA registers are modeled
   (else nordic,nrf-spim times out "transfer complete").
3. FICR DEVICEID read as zero -> L2 refused to start ("hardware ID is all
   zeros"). Tagged DEVICEID; per-node in the multi-node harnesses so each node
   gets a unique EUI-64/IPv6 (verified distinct fe80:: addresses).
4. Renode SPIM easyDMA never calls FinishTransmission between transfers, so the
   SX1262.cs opcode state machine never reset -> no TX/RX. Fixed by resetting on
   CS (cs-gpios) deassert.
Plus: renode_console.overlay routes console RTT->UARTE0 (r7h4.5.1); latent
harness bugs fixed (project_root off-by-one, pytest_addoption in conftest,
async fixture decorator, sim.get_stats() -> metrics.transmissions).

RESULTS:
- Boot: PASS (console on uart0, "Booting Zephyr OS", puck starts).
- SPI: PASS (real SX1262 opcodes reach the peripheral).
- L2 init: PASS (lora_l2 initialized, IPv6 link-local derived, unique per node).
- LoRa TX via lichen-sim: PASS (metrics.transmissions>0, correct airtime).
- LoRa RX into firmware: NOT WORKING. sim->bridge delivery works but SX1262.cs
  polls RX only once at SetRx (needs continuous polling + RxDone IRQ) and the
  bridge/sim time-model doesn't integrate cleanly. Follow-ups: r7h4.6, r7h4.7.
- RAK4631: NOT functional in Renode (T-Echo-specific platform). Follow-up r7h4.9.

pytest lichen/boards/renode/nrf52840_lichen/test_mesh.py: 2 passed (boot + TX).
CI integration deferred to r7h4.8.
close_reason: Validation performed on Renode 1.16.1 (arm64). Fixed 4 blocking platform/peripheral bugs (spi1, easyDMA, FICR DEVICEID, CS-framing) + console overlay + latent harness bugs. Verified: boot, SPI, L2 init (unique per-node IPv6), LoRa TX to lichen-sim. test_mesh.py boot+TX pass. RX-into-firmware and RAK4631 not functional - tracked by follow-ups r7h4.6/.7/.9; CI by r7h4.8. Results documented in README + bead notes.
